seoTitle: Load Snapshots Into an Existing GoHighLevel Account
metaDescription: Learn how to import a GoHighLevel snapshot into an existing sub-account to quickly apply pre-built funnels, workflows, and settings.
intro: Snapshots let you bring pre-built funnels, workflows, and settings into an existing GoHighLevel account rather than building everything from scratch. This overview covers the general process of loading a snapshot into an account that's already set up and in use.

steps.heading: Access the snapshot import option
steps.body: Navigate to the area of your GoHighLevel account where snapshots can be imported into an existing sub-account.

steps.heading: Select the snapshot to load
steps.body: Choose the specific snapshot you want to import, whether it's one you've created or one shared with you.

steps.heading: Review what the snapshot will add
steps.body: Check what funnels, workflows, and settings the snapshot includes before applying it, since it will be merged into your existing setup.

steps.heading: Load the snapshot
steps.body: Import the snapshot into your account and allow the process to complete.

steps.heading: Verify the results
steps.body: Review your account afterward to confirm the snapshot's funnels, workflows, and settings were applied correctly alongside your existing setup.

faq.question: Will loading a snapshot overwrite my existing account?
faq.answer: Snapshots are generally merged into an account, but it's important to review what the snapshot contains before applying it to avoid unwanted overlaps.

faq.question: Can I load a snapshot into an account that already has funnels?
faq.answer: Yes, this tutorial specifically addresses loading a snapshot into an existing GoHighLevel account rather than a fresh one.

faq.question: Where do snapshots come from?
faq.answer: They can be ones you've built yourself or shared by another user, such as an agency or consultant.

faq.question: How do I know what a snapshot will change?
faq.answer: Reviewing the snapshot's contents before loading it helps you understand what funnels, workflows, or settings will be added.

faq.question: Who typically uses snapshots?
faq.answer: Agencies and GoHighLevel users who want to quickly apply pre-built systems to client or personal accounts.
